scenario: CompanyA is planning to implement a solution based on Microsoft Dataverse with a model-driven app. You need to make sure that duplicate leads with the same first and last name are not created. You cannot use code.
why: Use an alternate key defined on the combination of first name and last name. An alternate key enforces uniqueness at the database level, so Dataverse blocks the creation of a second lead with the same first and last name - and it requires no code. A duplicate detection rule only WARNS the user about potential duplicates (and can be bypassed/ignored) and does not run on API/integration creates, so it does not strictly prevent duplicates. A plug-in would work but requires code, which is not allowed. A duplicate detection job scans EXISTING records to find duplicates after the fact; it does not prevent new duplicates from being created.
